seoTitle: How to Enforce Two-Step Verification in Google Workspace
metaDescription: Learn how to enable and enforce two-step verification for every user in Google Workspace using the Admin Console's security settings and policies.
intro: Two-step verification adds a critical layer of security to Google Workspace by requiring a second identity check beyond just a password. This guide covers turning on 2SV through the Admin Console's Security settings and enforcing it across all users, either immediately or after a grace period. It's meant for IT admins, school administrators, and business owners who need to protect Gmail, Drive, and other Workspace services from unauthorized access.

steps.heading: Open the Security settings in Admin Console
steps.body: Log in as an admin and navigate to the Security section where two-step verification controls live.

steps.heading: Turn on two-step verification for your domain
steps.body: Enable 2SV as an option so users in your organization can start setting it up on their accounts.

steps.heading: Set an enforcement policy
steps.body: Choose whether to require 2SV immediately or allow a grace period so users have time to set up their second verification method.

steps.heading: Guide users through setting up their devices
steps.body: Let users know they'll need to register a phone or authentication method, since this is required once enforcement takes effect.

steps.heading: Plan for recovery options
steps.body: Set up backup codes and recovery paths in advance for users who might lose access to their second verification method.

faq.question: What happens if a user loses their two-step verification device?
faq.answer: Admins can help them regain access using backup codes or by resetting their 2SV enrollment from the Admin Console.

faq.question: Can I enforce two-step verification gradually instead of all at once?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Workspace lets you set a grace period so users have time to enroll before enforcement becomes mandatory.

faq.question: Does two-step verification protect all Workspace apps or just Gmail?
faq.answer: It protects the entire Google Workspace login, which covers Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and other connected services under one sign-in.

faq.question: Are backup codes required for two-step verification?
faq.answer: They're not strictly required to enable 2SV, but Google recommends setting them up as a recovery option in case a user's primary method is unavailable.
